user_query:
Please read input/learner_profile.json, input/previous_session_keywords.txt, and input/format_instructions.md. Then create a complete JLPT N3 Japanese daily practice session tailored to that profile and preferences. Deliverables:

1) Write the full session to output/session.md using these exact section headings in this order:
- "## Vocabulary (10 items)" — number items 1. to 10. and include for each: word with furigana in parentheses, romaji, English meaning, one Japanese example sentence with English translation, and a brief memory tip.
- "## Grammar pattern of the day" — include structure, plain-English explanation, 3 examples (easy→hard), common mistakes, and a brief contrast with a similar pattern.
- "## Kanji focus (3 kanji)" — for each: character, on/kun readings, stroke count, two compounds, and one example sentence.
- "## Reading passage" — include a label "Reading (JP):" followed by the Japanese passage, then a label "Translation (EN):" followed by the full English translation. Also list exactly 3 bullet-point key vocabulary/grammar highlights from the passage.
- "## Listening/speaking prompt" — include a short scenario, a 2–4 exchange sample dialogue (JP with EN translation), 3 speaking prompts to practice, and suggested response vocabulary.
- "## Quick quiz (5 questions)" — format questions exactly as lines starting with Q1:, Q2:, Q3:, Q4:, Q5:. After the questions, place a divider line consisting of exactly five dashes (-----) on its own line, then an "Answers" subsection with lines A1:, A2:, A3:, A4:, A5: containing the correct answers.
- "## Cultural note" — 2–3 sentences only.

2) Also write these support files:
- output/vocab.csv with header: word,furigana,romaji,meaning and exactly 10 data rows matching the Vocabulary section items.
- output/kanji.json containing an array of 3 objects (one per kanji) with keys: character, on (array of strings), kun (array of strings), stroke_count (integer), compounds (array of exactly 2 strings), example (string).
- output/metadata.json containing: level (must be "N3"), vocabulary_count, kanji_count, quiz_count, focus_topics (copy from input/learner_profile.json), avoided_terms (copy from input/previous_session_keywords.txt, as an array), format_version (string), and a brief compliance_notes string.

3) Requirements you must follow:
- Level is N3. Include kanji focus (3 kanji).
- Weave at least one of the focus_topics into the reading passage and at least one example sentence.
- Do not include any words from avoided_terms anywhere in output/session.md.
- Ensure output/vocab.csv matches the 10 vocabulary items in the session.
- In Reading passage, ensure Translation (EN) is between 80 and 200 words.
- Use clear, consistent formatting exactly as specified so automated checks can find the sections and counts.

Write all outputs under output/ and do not write anywhere else.
